steps.title: Find the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses: many people were skeptical, and historians have determined that Shakespeare's company performed Cardenio. When independent clauses are joined with the coordinating conjunction but, a comma belongs before the conjunction.

steps.title: Test the punctuation
steps.content: Choice A lacks the needed comma before but. Choice C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. Choice D gives no punctuation or conjunction between the clauses. The answer is B.
